mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate at or near zero (FEDFUNDS < 0.25%) signals an extended monetary accommodation regime. Under this regime, equity valuations expand due to compressed discount rates and reduced opportunity cost of equity relative to cash. This mechanism operates continuously when the zero lower bound constraint is binding.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.12% as of April 2015 indicates sustained zero-rate regime post-2008
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of observations when FEDFUNDS < 0.25%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window during zero-rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) below 5.5% signals tightening labor market conditions and rising wage pressures, which compress corporate profit margins and reduce equity valuations. The mechanism triggers when unemployment crosses below the 5.5% threshold, indicating late-cycle labor market stress.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 5.4% as of April 2015, approaching threshold
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances when UNRATE < 5.5%, or the correlation between UNRATE and forward returns becomes positive and statistically significant (p < 0.05) over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Real retail sales (RSAFS) growth accelerates above its 3-month moving average, signaling strengthening consumer demand and corporate revenue growth expectations. This mechanism is always-on and predicts positive equity returns as stronger sales translate to earnings growth.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 436,772 million dollars as of April 2015
failure_condition: Rank IC between RSAFS growth and 20-day forward returns falls below 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or SPX produces negative returns in more than 50% of months with positive RSAFS growth.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) decelerates month-over-month while remaining above trend, signaling a supply-side slowdown that precedes demand weakness. This threshold-triggered mechanism predicts negative equity returns as forward earnings expectations decline.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month growth falls below 0.1% for two consecutive months
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 65% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window during supply deceleration periods.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) move in opposite directions, with HOUST declining while RSAFS remains stable or rising. This divergence signals a demand composition shift away from housing-sensitive sectors toward consumer goods, reducing overall equity market returns due to sector rotation and construction employment weakness.
candidate_trigger: HOUST 3-month growth rate falls below -5% while RSAFS 3-month growth remains positive
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 926k units as of March 2015
regime_hint: demand composition shift away from housing
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances, or the interaction term (HOUST decline × RSAFS stability) shows Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth decelerates below 150k jobs per month while inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerates above 2.5% year-over-year, creating a stagflationary signal. This interaction-driven mechanism predicts negative equity returns as the Fed faces pressure to tighten despite weakening labor demand.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month average growth < 150k AND CPIAUCSL year-over-month growth > 2.5%
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the joint interaction signal shows Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The spread between inflation (CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth) and the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) widens to more than 2.5 percentage points, signaling real interest rates are deeply negative. This regime-conditional mechanism predicts positive equity returns in the post-crisis recovery regime as negative real rates compress equity risk premiums.
candidate_trigger: Inflation rate minus FEDFUNDS > 2.5 percentage points
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.12% and CPIAUCSL inflation near 0% as of April 2015
regime_hint: post-crisis recovery with negative real rates
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances, or Rank IC between the real rate spread and forward returns falls below 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerates to below 2% annualized rate while housing starts (HOUST) remain elevated above 900k units, signaling a demand composition shift toward non-housing sectors with lower equity sensitivity. This regime-conditional mechanism predicts negative equity returns in the late-cycle expansion regime.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized growth rate < 2% AND HOUST > 900k units
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 16,304.8 billion as of Q1 2015
fact:HOUST at 926k units as of March 2015
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 65% of triggered instances, or the interaction signal shows Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window during late-cycle periods.
